etymology.description: 大 (person) + 火 (fire) + 廿 (mouth screaming) = a person being burned in a sacrificial ceremony
etymology.interpretation: The character 堇 (jǐn) symbolizes a ritual involving burning a person as a sacrifice, reflected by the components 大 (dà, 'person'), 火 (huǒ, 'fire'), and 廿 (niàn, 'mouth'), which together suggest the scene of an ancient ceremony intended to invoke rain by offering burnt earth.
